reviewFocus: Abstain when the supplied synthetic labels are too opaque to accept or reject the main claim.
Emit stable generic finding keys only when a support gap can be judged from the packet.
Use stable generic evidence-request keys when the caller must supply specific missing evidence.
Keep evidence retrieval, freshness checks, approval, routing, and domain interpretation caller-owned.
outOfScope: Do not infer private sources, hidden approvals, real source freshness, or domain-specific policy from synthetic labels.
Do not approve publication, deployment, remediation, routing, retention, purchasing, or other external action.
Do not treat generic evidence labels as proof outside this caller-sanitized packet.
